facts.primaryTitle: The Candle That Kept the Werewolf Away
facts.setting: The old woman gave Nina one instruction: keep the candle burning until sunrise.
criticalEvents: The old woman gave Nina one instruction: keep the candle burning until sunrise.
When the flame died, something outside stood on two legs.
Nina Falk was a wildlife photographer sheltering from a blizzard when the story began in an isolated mountain cottage in the Carpathians.
The first reports did not describe an obvious monster or violent attack.
facts.threat: The old woman gave Nina one instruction: keep the candle burning until sunrise.
facts.primaryReveal: The final warning is therefore simple: when the same impossible detail appears twice, do not wait for a third occurrence to prove that it is real.
facts.finalConsequence: The final warning is therefore simple: when the same impossible detail appears twice, do not wait for a third occurrence to prove that it is real.
facts.unresolvedQuestion: Did Nina Falk defeat the thing at the centre of the story, or did the encounter simply teach it how to remain hidden?
